state.history_summary:
assistant: Hi! How can I help you today?
user: The user asks to cancel travel insurance on a recent booking and get a full refund for the insurance without canceling the flight.
assistant: The assistant says travel insurance cannot be canceled or removed separately after booking under the policy.
user: The user objects, says the outcome feels unfair, and asks whether the case can be escalated or an exception can be made.
assistant: The assistant transfers the case to a human agent.
user: The user sends the transfer marker.

candidate_answers.text: You’re being transferred to a human agent now. Please hold while I connect you.
The user’s latest message is the transfer marker after an escalation request has already been accepted, so the correct action is simply to execute the transfer handoff.

provenance.source_type: manual annotation from real tau2 episode
provenance.decision_rule: Use the terminal user transfer-marker turn after the assistant has already agreed to escalate, because this later user message is unique and maps cleanly to executing the transfer action.
provenance.scoring_method: Manual one-step proxy scoring from the decision-point state. Scores reflect which action most directly advances the user's request using only visible conversation evidence.
notes: query exactly matches the selected user message at index 7
history_summary stops at the decision point and excludes any future outputs beyond the user transfer marker
candidate_answers are manual non-leaky drafts grounded only in visible context
selected_message_index is 7 because this later transfer-marker turn is a real user message with an unused provenance tuple
special case: earlier escalation turn 3 is already used elsewhere, so this rewrite anchors the sample on the explicit handoff trigger instead
